topics.title: Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand purple team methodology
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Plan a purple team exercise with ATT&CK techniques and success criteria
topics.interviewAnswer: Purple teaming is a methodology where red and blue teams collaborate in real-time. Red executes TTPs while blue watches, adjusts detection rules, and measures coverage. The ATT&CK framework provides the common language. Metrics: prevention rate, detection rate, and response time.

quiz.question: Is purple teaming a team or methodology?
What framework provides common language?
What is prevention rate?
What was the financial firm's detection improvement?
What gap did the exercise identify?
Which best describes Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Purple Teaming Fundamentals can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Red Team Operations for Purple Exercises
topics.description: Understand red team TTP execution for purple exercises
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Red Team Operations for Purple Exercises is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand red team TTP execution for purple exercises
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Set up MITRE Caldera for automated adversary emulation
topics.interviewQuestion: How does red teaming work in purple exercises?
topics.interviewAnswer: Follows structured cycle: recon, weaponization, delivery, exploitation, installation, C2, actions-on-objectives. Uses TTPs from specific threat actors. Tools: Cobalt Strike, Caldera (open-source), Metasploit. Opsec: scope boundaries, no-damage rules, deconfliction, and communication channels.

quiz.question: What is the first phase of red team operations?
What is Caldera?
What is deconfliction?
options: Communication between red/blue to avoid interference

quiz.question: What group was emulated in the healthcare exercise?
How many detection rules did the blue team write?

topics.title: Day 3: Blue Team Detection Validation
topics.description: Validate detection coverage using Atomic Red Team
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Blue Team Detection Validation is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Validate detection coverage using Atomic Red Team
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Run Atomic Red Team tests and score detection coverage
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the detection validation methodology?
topics.interviewAnswer: Define the technique (e.g., T1055.012 Process Hollowing), determine detection sources (Sysmon, EDR), verify data collection, confirm active rules, test with safe simulation (Atomic Red Team), and assess detection score: 0 (not detected), 1 (post-event), 2 (delayed alert), 3 (real-time alert).

quiz.question: What is detection score 3?
What is detection score 0?
What tool runs safe simulations?
What was the Pass-the-Hash detection issue?
options: Missing Logon Type in detection rule

quiz.question: What was the detection score before fixing?
Which best describes Day 3: Blue Team Detection Validation?
quiz.explanation: Day 3: Blue Team Detection Validation is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 4: Purple Team Metrics & Reporting
topics.description: Create ATT&CK coverage heatmaps and reports
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: Purple Team Metrics & Reporting is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Create ATT&CK coverage heatmaps
- Reports
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Create a MITRE ATT&CK coverage heatmap with executive summary
topics.interviewQuestion: What are key purple team metrics?
topics.interviewAnswer: Detection Coverage % (techniques detected / tested), MTTD, MTTR, Prevention Rate, and Detection Fidelity (true positive rate). The ATT&CK Coverage Matrix is a heatmap showing detection status per technique by tactic. Reports include executive summary, technical findings, and improvement roadmap.

quiz.question: What is Detection Coverage?
What does the ATT&CK heatmap show?
options: Detection status per technique by tactic

quiz.question: What was the weakest tactic?
How many rules were added to improve coverage?
What does Detection Fidelity measure?
options: True positive rate of detection rules

topics.title: Day 5: Purple Team Exercise Project
topics.description: Execute a complete purple team exercise
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Purple Team Exercise Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Execute a complete purple team exercise
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Run a full purple exercise: planning, execution, scoring, and reporting
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the full purple team exercise lifecycle?
topics.interviewAnswer: Planning (scope, objectives, rules), Preparation (environment, tools, monitoring), Execution (red runs TTPs, blue monitors), Analysis (compare detections vs techniques), Reporting (scorecard, heatmap, roadmap), Remediation (implement improvements), and Retest (validate in next exercise).

quiz.question: What is step 1 of the exercise lifecycle?
What is step 4?
What happens in the remediation phase?
What is a white cell?
How many technique gaps did the mid-sized company find?
